2603.14096.title: Concisely Explaining the Doubt: Minimum-Size Abductive Explanations for Linear Models with a Reject Option
2603.14096.paper_summary: Trustworthiness in artificial intelligence depends not only on what a model decides, but also on how it handles and explains cases in which a reliable decision cannot be made. In critical domains such as healthcare and finance, a reject option allows the model to abstain when evidence is insufficient, making it essential to explain why an instance is rejected in order to support informed human intervention. In these settings, explanations must not only be interpretable, but also faithful to the underlying model and computationally efficient enough to support real-time decision making. Abductive explanations guarantee fidelity, but their exact computation is known to be NP-hard for many classes of models, limiting their practical applicability. Computing \textbf{minimum-size} abductive explanations is an even more challenging problem, as it requires reasoning not only about fidelity but also about optimality. Prior work has addressed this challenge in restricted settings, including log-linear-time algorithms for computing minimum-size abductive explanations in linear models without rejection, as well as a polynomial-time method based on linear programming for computing abductive explanations, without guarantees of minimum size, for linear models with a reject option. In this work, we bridge these lines of research by computing minimum-size abductive explanations for linear models with a reject option. For accepted instances, we adapt the log-linear algorithm to efficiently compute optimal explanations. For rejected instances, we formulate a 0-1 integer linear programming problem that characterizes minimum-size abductive explanations of rejection. Although this formulation is NP-hard in theory, our experimental results show that it is consistently more efficient in practice than the linear-programming-based approach that does not guarantee minimum-size explanations.
2603.14096.paper_summary_zh: 信任度在人工智慧中不僅取決於模型的決策，還取決於它如何處理和解釋無法做出可靠決策的情況。<lb/>在醫療和金融等關鍵領域，拒絕選項允許模型在證據不足時選擇不作決策，因此解釋為何某個實例被拒絕對於支持知情的人類干預至關重要。<lb/>在這些情境中，解釋不僅必須可解釋，還必須忠實於底層模型，並且計算效率足夠高以支持實時決策。<lb/>推理解釋保證了忠實性，但對於許多類型的模型，其精確計算被認為是 NP-hard，這限制了它們的實際應用。<lb/>計算 \textbf{最小尺寸} 的推理解釋是一個更具挑戰性的問題，因為它需要不僅考慮忠實性，還要考慮最優性。<lb/>先前的研究在受限的環境中解決了這一挑戰，包括針對沒有拒絕選項的線性模型計算最小尺寸推理解釋的對數線性時間算法，以及基於線性規劃的多項式時間方法，用於計算具有拒絕選項的線性模型的推理解釋，但不保證最小尺寸。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們通過計算具有拒絕選項的線性模型的最小尺寸推理解釋來橋接這些研究方向。<lb/>對於被接受的實例，我們調整對數線性算法以有效計算最佳解釋。<lb/>對於被拒絕的實例，我們制定了一個 0-1 整數線性規劃問題，該問題描述了拒絕的最小尺寸推理解釋。<lb/>儘管這一公式在理論上是 NP-hard，但我們的實驗結果顯示，在實踐中它的效率始終優於不保證最小尺寸解釋的基於線性規劃的方法。
2603.14096.author: Gleilson Pedro Fernandes et.al.
2603.14096.authors: Gleilson Pedro Fernandes, Thiago Alves Rocha
